Changing MS2_DUP_bl and OCB_DUN_bond lengths increases the separation between dummy beads (DUP and DUN), while increasing charge magnitude enhances the dipole moment (μ = q × d). These parameters are orthogonal drivers: Lennard-Jones parameters control thermodynamic properties while dummy bead geometry and charge control electronic properties.

proposal.reasoning: The diagnostic reveals a critical contradiction: density is 7% too low (poor packing/excessive excluded volume) while surface tension is 5-14% too high with an unphysical positive temperature coefficient (excessive electrostatic cohesion at the interface). The stuck counter at 8 indicates we are in a local minimum and need aggressive exploration. Following diagnostic recommendations, I must simultaneously decrease Rmin parameters (to improve packing) and reduce charge magnitudes (to correct surface tension temperature dependence). Given tight parameter boundaries (Rmin can only decrease 1.5% to 5.07, charge can decrease 5% to 0.28), I will explore the full allowable range with 4 diverse strategies: (1) Aggressive reduction of both Rmin and charge with neutral epsilon to test the core hypothesis; (2) Aggressive reduction of both with increased epsilon to test if stronger dispersion can compensate for charge-reduced cohesion and further boost density; (3) Aggressive charge reduction with moderate Rmin reduction to isolate the charge effect on surface tension; (4) Aggressive Rmin reduction with moderate charge reduction and moderate epsilon increase for a balanced approach. This parallel exploration tests the decoupling of packing (Rmin) from interfacial electrostatics (charge) while respecting the constraint that dipole geometry remains fixed.

hypothesis.scientific_rationale: Reducing Rmin from 5.15/2.7 to 5.07/2.67 decreases excluded volume, enabling tighter molecular packing to correct the 7% density deficit. Simultaneously, reducing charge magnitude from 0.295 to 0.28 (or 0.285) weakens the excessive electrostatic contribution to surface tension, which should correct the unphysical positive temperature coefficient (surface tension should decrease with temperature, not increase). These changes are complementary: Rmin primarily affects bulk density through excluded volume, while charge primarily affects interfacial properties through electrostatic cohesion. By decoupling these parameters, we can resolve the contradiction of low density with high surface tension.
hypothesis.expected_benefit: This will improve density toward the target 1.095 g/cm³ by reducing excluded volume, reduce surface tension toward 42.09 mN/m at 298K, and correct the temperature dependence so surface tension decreases appropriately to 40.05 mN/m at 323K. The heat of vaporization, currently slightly high (2-3%), should remain stable or slightly decrease due to reduced charge interactions, moving toward the target 12.648 kcal/mol.
hypothesis.test_method: The hypothesis will be tested by evaluating 4 parallel parameter sets that explore different combinations of Rmin reduction (5.07 vs 5.10 vs 5.12), charge reduction (0.28 vs 0.285 vs 0.295), and epsilon adjustments (current vs -0.40/-0.30 vs -0.42/-0.32). Success will be measured by simultaneous improvement in density (increase) and surface tension (decrease) while maintaining physical temperature dependence.
